_comment: Schema overrides — human corrections layered ON TOP of auto-generated schema-inferred.json. Anything not listed here uses the inferred value. Tables / columns are matched case-insensitively by Name.

Columns.Name: <ColumnName>
Columns.Role: primary_key | foreign_key | natural_key | label | soft_delete | date | audit
Columns.DateRole: created | modified | resolved | completed | due | started

Tables._note: Example 1 — TicketNumber has no DB-level UNIQUE constraint, so the heuristic misses it. Mark it as natural key here so 'find ticket TKT-2026-00123' resolves. Synonyms: Arabic forms so SchemaLinker.IsAnchor can lexically anchor an Arabic question on Tickets (the central entity) without depending on the bge-m3 embedding fallback. Merged additively with the auto-generated English synonyms.
Tables.Name: Tickets

Tables._note: Example 3 — give the LLM a richer description so retrieval / spec extraction picks the right table.
Tables.Name: CopilotTraceHistories
Tables.Description: Per-question trace records — one row per copilot answer with question, SQL, rows, timing, errors. Useful for debugging or analytics on copilot usage.

Tables._note: Bills — Status is DESCRIPTIVE here, not a default filter. A neutral value enumeration avoids the 7B bolting Status='Issued' onto totals it was never asked to filter.
Tables.Name: Bills
Tables.Description: Utility bill. TotalAmount is the billed amount (SYP). Status is a lifecycle state column with values Issued, Paid, Overdue, Disputed, Cancelled. Apply a Status filter ONLY when the question names a payment state — a total / sum / count / average of bills with no stated state spans ALL statuses. When you do filter payment state, use Status (e.g. Status='Paid'), never the PaidAt timestamp.
Tables.GrainNote: TotalAmount is the billed amount (SYP). The payment lifecycle is the Status column — use Status (not the PaidAt timestamp) for payment state.

Tables._note: Customers — Status is DESCRIPTIVE (value enumeration), not a default filter.
Tables.Name: Customers
Tables.Description: External utility subscriber. Status is a lifecycle state column with values Active, Suspended, Churned. Apply a Status filter ONLY when the question names a lifecycle state — a count / list of customers with no stated state spans ALL statuses. When you do filter lifecycle state, use Status (e.g. Status='Churned'), never the ChurnedAt timestamp. Soft-deleted customers have IsDeleted=1.
Tables.GrainNote: The customer lifecycle is the Status column — use Status (not the ChurnedAt timestamp) for lifecycle state.

Tables._note: Tariffs — EffectiveTo NULL means active (no end date).
Tables.Name: Tariffs
Tables.Description: Historical pricing per (ServiceType, Region). EffectiveTo IS NULL means the tariff is currently active. To find 'active tariffs' use 'EffectiveTo IS NULL'. To find 'tariffs that changed in 2025' use 'EffectiveFrom BETWEEN ...'.
Tables.GrainNote: One row per (ServiceType, Region) pricing period; EffectiveFrom and EffectiveTo bound the period.

Tables._note: TicketCategories — hierarchical. ParentCategoryId NULL means Primary, NOT NULL means Secondary.
Tables.Name: TicketCategories
Tables.Description: Hierarchical taxonomy of complaint types. ParentCategoryId IS NULL means the row is a Primary (top-level) category; ParentCategoryId IS NOT NULL means it is a Secondary (child of a Primary). Tier column also encodes this enum-wise. To list 'primary categories' use ParentCategoryId IS NULL. To count 'secondary categories' use ParentCategoryId IS NOT NULL.
Tables.GrainNote: ParentCategoryId IS NULL marks a Primary (top-level) category; ParentCategoryId IS NOT NULL marks a Secondary (child) category. The Tier column encodes the same level.

Tables._note: Outages — EndedAt NULL means still ongoing. Filter on Cause/Severity for typical analyst questions.
Tables.Name: Outages
Tables.Description: Service outage events. EndedAt IS NULL means the outage is ongoing (still active). Use DATEDIFF(hour, StartedAt, EndedAt) for duration (only for resolved outages). Cause is an enum: Equipment, Weather, PipeBurst, FiberCut, etc. Severity: Minor, Moderate, Major, Critical.
Tables.GrainNote: Each row is one outage event; for an ended outage the duration is DATEDIFF(hour, StartedAt, EndedAt).

Tables._note: Regions — hierarchical (Governorate vs District) via ParentRegionId. Describe the grain; do NOT prescribe a ParentRegionId filter for plain 'region' questions.
Tables.Name: Regions
Tables.Description: Hierarchical geography via ParentRegionId: a row with ParentRegionId IS NULL is a Governorate (14); a row with ParentRegionId IS NOT NULL is a District. GRAIN: Tickets and Customers reference a District (RegionId points at a leaf district); to roll a Ticket/Customer up to its governorate, join Regions to its parent on ParentRegionId. Departments reference a Governorate directly. Do NOT add a ParentRegionId or RegionType filter unless the question explicitly asks for governorates or districts; plain 'regions' means all rows.
Tables.GrainNote: ParentRegionId IS NULL marks a Governorate (14 total); ParentRegionId IS NOT NULL marks a District. Tickets.RegionId and Customers.RegionId point at a leaf District; joining Regions to its parent on ParentRegionId rolls a Ticket or Customer up to its Governorate. Departments reference a Governorate directly.

Tables._note: AspNetUsers — the PRIMARY 'users/staff/agents' table. Arabic forms so an Arabic question ('كم عدد الموظفين') anchors here lexically and not on Customers (external subscribers).
Tables.Name: AspNetUsers

Tables._note: Phase 06 Arabic synonyms. The blocks below add Arabic forms for the 15 new entities. Synonyms are MERGED with the auto-generated English ones (additive, not replacing). The English forms come from SchemaInferenceGenerator's TableSynonymDictionary; Arabic comes from here.
Tables.Name: Currencies
